\- FEBRUARY 5th 2016 -
THANK YOU FOR YOUR CONTINUED SUPPORT
| Dear Peter,

I want to let you know how honored and excited we are to have your support. Together, we will make this country stronger and better than ever by making Mr. Trump our next president. One of my favorite parts of this campaign experience has been visiting each state and meeting thousands of supporters who share Mr. Trump's dreams for this great nation. Like you, many citizens are anxious to elect a leader who will secure our borders, build our military, defend our Second Amendment rights, and repair our economy. My faith in the United States of America is revitalized at every Trump rally.

On Tuesday, we held a huge rally in Little Rock, Arkansas where Mr. Trump was greeted by more than 12,000 supporters. Yesterday, we held two events in New Hampshire and today, we are heading down to Florence, South Carolina. We are looking forward to the primary election in New Hampshire on Tuesday**\---** Mr. Trump is well-loved and respected in the state, and he is a huge fan of the people of New Hampshire (which makes me especially proud as a Granite State native!). Thank you for your continued support--- I’m proud to call you a teammate.

Sincerely,

Corey

Corey R. Lewandowski
Campaign Manager

name: Jonathan Slemrod, MarcoRubio.com
subject: Marco’s record
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Marco Rubio for President
body:
| | | | | | | | [](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/46107096:6w57vdrNz:m:1:1349778030:C78B571D813D98671D993AB0EACA66AD:r)
---
| | | | Peter, As the primary season heats up, there's a lot of talk about various candidates' records -- what they've done in office, what it means about what they value. I wanted to give you a quick primer on Marco's record of accomplishment and what it says about him. First off, in office, he's always been unabashedly conservative: At all three levels of government, from the West Miami city council to the Senate, he's stood up for free markets, property rights, and conservative values. In the Senate, he's distinguished himself from some other conservative senators by achieving real legislative results, not just grandstanding. The most prominent example is the fact that he led the charge on the most successful thing Republicans have done to undermine ObamaCare: blocking the Obama Administration's attempted bailout of insurance companies. Marco's effort has saved taxpayers billions, and, because insurers are losing so much under the law, it may unravel entirely without the bailout. One conservative health care expert summed it up this way: Marco "has done more than pretty much anyone to actually push back against Obamacare." He is a highly regarded leader on foreign policy issues, and has successfully pushed through new sanctions on Hezbollah and Venezuela -- in the latter case, against strong opposition from the Obama Administration. He led the passage of the Girls Count Act, which will combat sex trafficking by using U.S. foreign-aid know how to help ensure women and girls in poor countries are registered at birth. Marco's record as Speaker of the Florida House was impressive, too: He balanced the budget both years without raising taxes, and earned a 100 percent rating from the Florida Chamber of Commerce and the National Federation of Independent Business. And he got _**a lot**_ else done: He won the speakership on a platform of 100 ideas that he gathered from talking to Floridians across the state, and dozens of them eventually became law. The victories included things like encouraging innovation in schools, moving to pay health care providers based on value provided, privatizing parts of the state's transportation system, modernizing hurricane insurance, and more. When the Supreme Court's infamous Kelo case massively expanded the power of governments to seize private property via eminent domain, Marco led the successful effort to curtail eminent domain power in Florida, protecting people from abuse. That's a whole lot of policy talk, I know -- and as Marco's said, the most important accomplishments he has, and will have, come not from his work as a politician but as a husband and a father. That's the most important job he'll ever have. But I'll sum it up this way: No one in the Republican field has dealt a blow to ObamaCare like he has, and no one in the field has demonstrated better understanding and judgment about foreign affairs at a dangerous time for America. If you'd like to learn more about Marco's stance on the issues and Marco's record of getting things done on them, check out **[MarcoRubio.com](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/46107096:6w57vdrNz:m:1:1349778030:C78B571D813D98671D993AB0EACA66AD:r)**. Thanks for reading, and thank you for your support, Jonathan SlemrodPolicy DirectorMarco Rubio for President

name: heidicruz267@gmail.com
subject: Quick question
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CRUZ FOR
body:
CRUZ FOR PRESIDENT

I know it's the weekend -- so I'll make this quick. I just wanted
to reconnect because your name came up once again when we were
reviewing some notes about prospective Cruz Team members.

Hi Friend,

I know it's the weekend -- so I'll make this quick.

I just wanted to reconnect because your name came up once again
when we were reviewing some notes about prospective Cruz Team
members.

After our big win in Iowa I hope you will reconsider becoming the
newest Cruz for President Team member?

Ted is hoping you will join before tonight's debate.

Friend, please consider this your personal invitation -- I'm
hoping you'll accept because 2016 is critical for America's
future.

Ted asked me to reach out to you and a few other of our best
supporters. You are the type of person we really need on our side
to keep Ted's momentum going.

You see: Ted is "all in" to reignite liberty and bring
conservative, Constitutional leadership back to our nation. To
put it briefly -- we need you standing with us.

Here are the highlights:

**
Conservatives absolutely must win the White House in this
pivotal election year.

**
Ted is one of the leaders in conservative movement -- with
some of our nation's brightest minds and proven policy solutions
to make conservative principles triumph in 2016.

**
Your Cruz for President Team membership will make you a
vital part of the real, grass-roots conservative movement that
can transform America.

I'm not contacting all our supporters to join -- just those who
Ted and I believe will be the best. And your name keeps coming
up in conversation Friend.

If you have already joined -- and our emails have crossed in
cyberspace -- please forgive me. But if you haven't, I'm worried
that you might not have received my messages.

You can become a Cruz Team member with a gift as little as $3.
If you join today, I will be sure to let Ted know before he takes
the stage in tonight's debate.

I know you're busy, and I know this is the weekend. BUT will you
take a moment right now to make a real difference for America in
2016? Become a Cruz Team member today.

We've made it easy for you. Click here now to join!
Thanks for considering my invitation.

Warmly,
Heidi Cruz
Cruz for President

P.S. Tonight is critical to Ted's campaign -- I would love
nothing more than to let him know before he takes the stage that
you are on board. Please join today.

name: Ben Carson (iPhone)
subject: Gut check
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Carson America, Inc
body:
|
Peter,

Good afternoon from New Hampshire, where I'm getting ready for tonight's debate.

It's scheduled to begin at 8pm ET on ABC, and I hope you're planning to watch because with the New Hampshire primary just 3 days away, it may be the most important debate yet.

I plan to talk about the need for vision, for integrity and for accountability in Washington, D.C.

As you've probably seen by now, my opponents will stop at nothing in attacking me. There's no doubt that arrows will be thrown tonight in every direction, but my goal is to stay focused on what matters most -- returning "We the People" to the pinnacle of power in America.

With just 3 days to go until the New Hampshire primary, it's gut check time, and I need all of my supporters around the country to stand with me.

[Before the debate begins, will you donate just $3?](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/33376845:WIgF8TCNE:m:1:876315805:08D6F0F88BC04C778B7D3C10280DE5BB:r)

I want to come out of tonight's debate ready to push forward in the strongest way possible.

Knowing that you'll be watching, and that I have your support, will truly mean a lot.

Thank you, and I'll be in touch soon.

-Ben

Sent from my iPhone

subject: Cup of Joe: February 6, 2016
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Joe Sestak
body:
www.JoeSestak.com
CUP OF JOE

February 6th, 2016
Weekly Newsletter

Good morning! I wanted to provide an update about what I've been up to this past week. Thanks so much for reading. I appreciate your support.

Watch: the First U.S. Senate Debate

On Sunday, I returned to Carnegie Mellon University -- where I taught as a professor -- to participate in the first U.S. Senate debate of this race. Click the video below to hear what I had to say about expanding access to health care, protecting our environment, and improving our national security.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yhHtMeyaLMU

Delco Democrats back Sestak for U.S. Senate

By Patti Mengers, Delaware County Daily Times

Delegates to the Delaware County Democratic Committee's annual nominating convention, staged Sunday at Strath Haven Middle School in Nether Providence, threw full support behind former U.S. Rep. Joe Sestak of Edgmont as their nominee to unseat Republican U.S. Sen. Pat Toomey in November.

In 2010, the Delaware County Democrats also endorsed Sestak over the now-late Sen. Arlen Specter, who had switched back to the Democratic Party after serving as a moderate Republican in the Senate for 30 years. Sestak won the primary, but Toomey beat the Springfield native by about 2 percent of the vote in the general election.

"We are incredibly excited for Joe Sestak to take up our party's mantle against Pat Toomey and the Republican right‐wing extremists in Washington," said Delaware County Democratic Committee Chairman David Landau on Monday afternoon. "Given how close he came to winning a tough election six years ago, we're proud to see Joe continuing to fight for the middle class in Delaware County and look forward to seeing him back in Washington, serving in the Senate."

Sestak, a retired Navy admiral who in the last year has walked more than 422 miles across the state campaigning for Toomey's seat, had represented Pennsylvania's 7th District in Congress from 2007 until 2011 when he left to run against Toomey the first time.

"I love Delaware County where I was born, raised, and served, and Lord willing, hope to serve again -- Democrat, Republican, Independent, everyone," Sestak said on Monday morning.

http://www.delcotimes.com/article/DC/20160201/NEWS/160209966

Traveling All Across the Commonwealth

I love meeting people. It is what I enjoy most about this campaign -- hearing the hopes and concerns of Pennsylvanians all across the state. This week, I listened to people all across the Commonwealth: Elk, Clarion, Allegheny, Bucks, Chester, and Montgomery counties.

Sestak calls for expanded health care coverage

By Rick Kauffman, Delaware County Daily Times

UPPER DARBY >> Throughout his campaign for the Democratic nomination for U.S. Senate, which included a walk across Pennsylvania last fall, former U.S. Rep. Joe Sestak has championed the Affordable Care Act and has pushed for more comprehensive coverage, especially in regards to health care for children.

On Wednesday, the former United States Navy admiral held a roundtable discussion at Five Points Coffee in Upper Darby to call to action a more profound focus on expanding services and research for autism spectrum disorder.

"We need a call to arms," said Sestak of Edgmont, who represented the 7th Congressional District from 2007 to 2011. "That is the purpose of this."

Sestak faces Braddock Mayor John Fetterman and Katie McGinty, the heavily favored former chief of staff to Gov. Tom Wolf, in the April 26 primary election. The winner will face incumbent U.S. Sen. Pat Toomey, R-Pa., in the fall.

In attendance at Sestak's Wednesday event were parents of individuals living with the disorder, non-profit organizers and concerned citizens who contributed personal experiences in a desire to make the disorder that affects one in 45 children in the United States a topic on the forefront of updated legislation.

The passing of the Affordable Care Act left the states to determine the levels of implementation. In Pennsylvania, the act does not mandate the applied behavioral analysis, which determines the nature and severity of a person within the spectrum, as well as other developmental and behavioral therapies on all insurance policies, whereas ABA is mandated through Medicaid.

Sestak suggested that Pennsylvania is in incorrect in its interpretation of the ACA.

"The enforcement and interpretation is not very good," Sestak said.

"(We need to) keep the ACA and make sure the essential benefit, which says that mental health and behavioral treatment are to be on par in health care, is fully implemented nationwide," Sestak said. "I don't think we should leave it to the individual states, I think we need more of a national (consistency)."

Many issues that people raised in the meeting had less to do with the initial diagnosis, which in its own right is vital to treatment and therapy, and more with those living with autism having the ability to transition into adulthood and self-sufficiency.

"I'm a father of an adult daughter with autism, we went through the whole system, but then what to do after that?" asked Jim Wurster, who started the non-profit New Avenue Foundation, which aids in employment and community inclusion.

Under ACA young adults are covered until the age of 26, and since autism spectrum disorder is classified as a pre-existing condition and therefore cannot be denied coverage under federal law, the question arises of what treatment is available for adults who are intelligent and adept workers but lack in the social skills necessary to find stable work.

"They all do have a value and they can give back to the community," Wurster said.

One man detailed his 30-year-old daughter who he said was on the "upper end of functionality" and takes after him, an engineer, in her adept understanding of computers. However, he said due to having "no social skills to get a job," she is in need of services to help her adapt to broad society.

"It's almost like low-hanging fruit, these people have a lot of skills, they could do something, but there's something else they need, services are needed to help them," he said.

Among a number of topics that Sestak touched on were expanding services to public-private partnerships and post-educational opportunities.

"This epidemic really came to the consciousness of America around the beginning of the last decade, so this increase of those on the autism spectrum are now the ones graduating," Sestak said. "Now all of a sudden it's here and we never had that large of a number before."

Heidi Morein, who currently attends Temple University for special education, was compelled to learn about the topic after her son was diagnosed with Asperger's Syndrome. She said that transitional services are among the most important issues facing people dealing with cognitive learning, social and developmental disabilities.

"If they get a job, they learn by trial and error how to work with other people," Morein said. "It develops their social skills, they gain maturity."

In asking Sestak what his immediate and long-term plans will be if elected to the U.S. Senate, preserving the ACA was at the top of the list.

"We need to make sure the essential benefit, which says that mental health and behavioral treatment are on part in health care, is fully implemented nationwide," he said. "I don't think we should leave it to the individual states, I think we need more of a national (consistency)."

http://www.delcotimes.com/general-news/20160203/sestak-calls-for-expanded-health-care-coverage

Stories from Those We Served

Single mothe r Valerie Jones's daughter Lucy has Asperger's on the autism spectrum, and my Congressional office worked to give her a full chance at the American Dream. Hear her story by clicking the video to the right!

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jdr7s4aSauQ

Thanks for reading -- and please click the "contribute" button to keep our momentum going.

Joe

body:
#
Team -
Bernie's starting to close the gap in the national polls after our amazing finish in Iowa.
If we can pull off a win in New Hampshire on Tuesday, our momentum will be huge going into contests in Nevada and South Carolina. Then on Super Tuesday, we'll have a real shot in the 11 states that all vote on March 1.
But this path to the nomination will only be possible if we have an incredible surge of volunteers contacting voters every single day from now until March 1.
**Can you attend a volunteer event for Bernie to help him win?** Here are some events happening near you:

**[Call voters from home:](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/content/phonebank?source=em160206 "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/content/phonebank?source=em160206")

What once seemed impossible is now within reach. But only if we do a massive amount of work. That's why we need you to sign up for as many volunteer shifts as you can.
In solidarity,
Team Bernie

body:
We've got to keep fighting | Friend -- I’m running for president because I want to make life better for American families, pure and simple. I'm not in the business of making promises I can't keep, and I'm not in this race to make a point. I'm here to make a difference.

**[But we're in an uphill battle in New Hampshire, and we're being outraised and outspent. Can you chip in $1 now to help close the gap?](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=12&ms=MjQ2NjE1NDcS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=NzQxMTM1OTg1S0&mt=1&rt=0)

** We're in a real fight for this nomination -- Senator Sanders outraised us by more than $5 million in January, and you don't win elections by losing the money race. If we're fortunate enough to win the primary, the fights to take on the Republicans and make the changes Americans need -- before and after Election Day -- will be even tougher.

But I'm ready. I've been fighting to make people's lives better for the last 40 years, and I'm not stopping now.

With just three days left until New Hampshire and our campaign being badly outraised, I've never needed your help more. **Chip in $1 right now, and show me you're ready to win these fights together:**
